title: Laboratory Measurements of MICA genetic variants and s-MICA
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6ff4g","text":"1. Study design","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8sok1","text":"A population-based case-control study of the exocrine pancreas was conducted using incident cases between 20 and 93 years old (mean age = 66 years), diagnosed between 1994 and 1998 in Minnesota. Pancreatic cancer cases (n=163) were ascertained from hospitals, and controls (n=542) were randomly selected from the general population and frequency matched to cases by age (within 5 years), sex and race. All participants were asked in person about demographics, cigarette smoking, physical activity, dietary and alcohol intake, medical history, and the type of treatment for cases. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"16jlo","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1c1k5","text":"2.\tS-MICA measurement ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"jgi8","text":"Blood was collected in 9-ml aliquots collected at the time of interview, and stored at -70C. After excluding participants for whom insufficient or no blood samples were available, a total of 163 cases and 542 controls were available for the current analysis (n=705).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c8tdl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b2hps","text":"S-MICA plasma levels were assessed in the Cytokine Reference Laboratory (UMN) using the Luminex method MICA enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) kit (R\u0026D Systems, MN).  This method uses a premixed Multi-Analyte Kit and s-MICA-specific antibodies were pre-coated onto magnetic microparticles. The kits were stored at -8C until use. S-MICA measurement were conducted following the manufacturer’s instructions (R\u0026D Systems, Minneapolis, MN), available at (https://www.rndsystems.com/products/human-magnetic-luminex-assay_lxsahm#product-details).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":458,"length":89}],"data":[]},{"key":"498fq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6milj","text":" The detection limit for MICA was set at 2 pg/ml, and all results greater than this value were considered positive. We detected s-MICA plasma levels in 85% of our participants: 20 of our 163 cases and 83 of our 542 controls had a MICA plasma level lower than 2 pg/ml.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ksh2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7lptc","text":"To evaluate laboratory precision and reproducibility, we inserted 3% duplicates blinded to laboratory personnel (CV% range: 0.77 – 18.9). All samples were assayed without the knowledge of case-control status. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c0ccj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fngde","text":"3.\tGenotyping","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cf1ap","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2m918","text":"This project involved amplifying exons 2-5 of the MICA gene from 705 samples (163 cases, 542 controls), either individually or in a multiplex PCR. These amplicons were then indexed (by sample) and sequenced as a pool. Sequencing was performed in the UMN Genomic Center using DNA previously isolated from peripheral blood lymphocytes and stored at -70C. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2niap","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1i65i","text":"Pilot phase:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b5vfg","text":"A small number of samples (8) were amplified for each exon individually (32 total reactions) and using a multiplexed PCR (8 reactions), then indexed, pooled, and sequenced using ~1/8 of a 2x300 bp MiSeq lane. We compared the relative coverage of each exon from either the individual or multiplexed reactions. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4knnv","text":"Production phase:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"48dqd","text":"In the production phase, all 705 samples were indexed and sequenced in a single 2x300 bp MiSeq lane providing ample read depth (~15,000,000 reads total, or an average of ~ 4,500 reads per amplicon per sample), even accounting for imperfect sample balance in the sequencing pool and reasonable abundance differences between multiplexed amplicons. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fbbl7","text":"To genotype exons 2-4 in the extracellular regions of MICA, genomic DNA region (1.9KB), were amplified by PCR and genotyped by high-resolution sequence-based typing using six nested primers. Each exon was individually sequenced on both DNA strands. Relevant alleles have been characterized in the IMGT/HLA Sequence Database, www.ebi.ac.uk/imgt/hla/. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1utgl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ajuc0","text":"Samples were amplified for each exon individually, using a multiplexed PCR approach, then indexed, pooled, and sequenced using a 2x300 bp MiSeq lane using the Illumina MiSeq Personal Sequencing platform in the University of Minnesota Genomic Center. The resulting reads were aligned to the hg19 reference genome using BWA-MEM (BWA version: 0.7.10-r789) and processed with GATK (GATK version: 3.3-0-g37228af) for base quality score recalibration and indel realignment. Variants outside the STR region were genotyped using the GATK HaplotypeCaller.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1fhfj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5kbuv","text":"Genotypes in the transmembrane region (exon 5) containing the tandem repeat were assigned by counting the number of times sequences from known alleles (A4-A10, and A5.1) were seen in reads overlapping the STR region. No sequencing errors were tolerated, and on average 89% of all reads were mapped directly to a known allele without sequencing error. An allele was considered for genotyping if its sequence represented \u003e20% of all allele counts. Samples with a single passing allele were called homozygous and samples with two passing alleles were called heterozygous. Samples with more than two candidate alleles above the 20% threshold were excluded. Four distinct alleles (A4, A5, A6, A9) corresponding to 4, 5, 6 and 9 GCT repeats, respectively, and the A5.1 allele with an additional insertion of a G base were identified. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"E44D00C058F149BD853F4896341A524E","url":"https://www.rndsystems.com/products/human-magnetic-luminex-assay_lxsahm#product-details)."}}}}
creator: Guillaume Onyeaghala
